# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for a Network Security Group that allows our remote development team to access Azure VMs. Include rules for RDP (port 3389), SSH (port 22), HTTP (port 80), and HTTPS (port 443) from any source (0.0.0.0/0). The team works globally and needs flexible access.
# Category: cloud_network_security

# ARM template for Network Security Group (NSG) to allow remote development of Azure VMs via RDP, SSH, HTTP & HTTPS from any source in global scope with dynamic IP allocation 

parameters:     # Parameters file is used when deploying the resources multiple times. Defines input variables/configurations at runtime by referencing a parameter group defined outside this template or inside another resource block like `variables`, 'outputs', etc.)  
- name: networkSecurityGroupName 
  type: string    # The Azure Resource Manager (ARM) ID of the Network Security Group that you want to create. This is required when deploying resources into an existing virtual network or subnet through a bring your own server model, such as with AKS clusters and cloud-based compute services like ACI & DCS
  default: myNSG   # Default value if not provided in parameters file    
- name: location   
  type: string      # The geographical location where the Network Security Group should reside. This is required when creating a NSG, and cannot be set afterwards (such as via AKS clusters or cloud services)      
  default: eastus   # Default value if not provided in parameters file    
- name: vnetName   
  type: string      # The Virtual Network to deploy the Nsg into. If this isn't specified, it will use a virtual network with the same name within Azure Resource Manager which has been created upfront or an existing one that you specify by ID if provided here    
- name: subnetName   
  type: string      # The Subnet in VNET to deploy Nsg into. If not defined then, it will use first available under Virtual Network and the same vNet can't be specified for a NSG as both are different from each other (can have one or more IP address space(s)).
- name: publicIpName  # The Public Ip Name if you want to create dynamic ip. It is optional but recommended when using custom subnet and VNET which may cause ARM policy error on first deployment due default value being static assigned in most cases (e.g., Standard SKU)    
- name: rdpRuleName  # The rule for RDP port, SSH & HTTP/HTTPS ports from any source   

resources:   # Define Resources to be created / updated here - this can include a Virtual Machine Deployment or even multiple Network Interface Attachments and Load Balancers etc.    
- type: Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups  // This is the ARM Resource Provider which defines how resources are defined, managed (with RBAC), connected to subscriptions / locations for managing those in a central location   --> It includes operations like creating or updating Nsg with rules including actions and protocols
... more details omitted ...      # Continue from previously commented section.  This will include the creation of Network Security Group using ARM template, along all necessary configurations such as RDP rule etc..    . Include also error handling if needed in deployment process for better user experience   (This is a high level and you'll need to replace dots omitted above with more details)
